name: runloop reads message stream (SQLite session)
trigger: runloop iteration: filterCompacted needs the message tail

messages.action: returns warm connection (or opens new + runs IntegrityChecker on first time)
SELECT id, role, time_created, finish, tokens_total FROM messages ORDER BY id
SELECT id, message_id, sequence, type, payload_json FROM parts WHERE message_id IN (...) ORDER BY message_id, sequence

invariants: exactly one SQL query per table (no per-message N+1)
tokens_total read from column, not estimated via JSON.stringify (DD-6)

name: runloop appends a streaming part (active write)
trigger: LLM stream emits a text-delta or tool-input-delta

messages.action: BEGIN; INSERT OR REPLACE INTO parts (id,message_id,sequence,type,payload_json) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?); COMMIT

invariants: WAL ensures the write is durable on commit (DR-1 / DR-2)
concurrent reader (e.g. TUI tail) sees consistent snapshot from prior commit

name: concurrent reader (admin panel) tails a session under active write

messages.action: opens short-lived read-only connection (separate from runloop's writer pool entry)
SELECT * with WAL snapshot at query begin time

invariants: DD-4: WAL multi-reader does not stall writer (or vice versa)
reader sees a consistent snapshot from the moment its connection began the read

name: dreaming mode migrates a legacy session
trigger: idle-sweep timer fires; daemon has been quiet for >= IDLE_THRESHOLD_MS

messages.action: scan storage root for <sid>/ directories without matching <sid>.db
LegacyStore.list(sessionID) and parts(messageID) for full session read
create <sid>.db.tmp; open SQLite handle; apply schema migrations to v1
BEGIN; insert all messages + parts; INSERT INTO meta VALUES ('schema_version','1'),('migrated_from_legacy', now), ('legacy_message_count', N); COMMIT
IntegrityChecker.run(<sid>.db.tmp) → expect 'ok' AND row count == legacy_message_count

invariants: DR-4: any crash before MSG6 leaves legacy intact + tmp orphaned (cleanup on next start)
DR-4: any crash between MSG6 and MSG7 leaves both formats; router prefers SQLite (Spec § dual-track Both formats present)
exactly one session migrated per tick; never preempts active write

name: user opens legacy session before dreaming mode reaches it
trigger: TUI / admin panel opens session whose <sid>/ is still legacy

messages.action: filesystem walk: list messages dir, read info.json + parts/*.json per message
returns MessageV2.WithParts[] (slow path; same shape as SqliteStore output)

invariants: DD-8: dreaming mode does NOT preempt-migrate to avoid blocking the user
session continues to be served by LegacyStore until idle sweep reaches it

name: DR-3: SQLite corruption detected on open
trigger: first open attempt of a corrupted <sid>.db

messages.action: throw StorageCorruptionError; daemon logs error; session refuses to load

invariants: DR-3: no auto-repair, no silent fallback to legacy (DD-13)
user-visible error path; manual recovery via session-inspect check + raw sqlite3

name: DR-5: schema migration on open with version bump
trigger: daemon expects schema v2; existing DB has v1

messages.action: open <sid>.db; SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='schema_version' → '1'
BEGIN; (migration SQL); UPDATE meta SET value='2' WHERE key='schema_version'; COMMIT
on success: open proceeds at v2
on failure: ROLLBACK; mark connection read-only; publish Bus event session.storage.migration_failed

invariants: DR-5: failed migration leaves DB at v1 (no half-migrated state)
every shipped migration has unit test forward + rollback (R-5)

name: session-inspect CLI debug

messages.action: stdout JSON / table; exit code reflects integrity_check

invariants: read-only by design; v1 does NOT expose write commands
works in both formats during dual-track period
